skill.name: critique
skill.description: Evaluate design effectiveness from a UX perspective. Assesses visual hierarchy, information architecture, emotional resonance, and overall design quality with actionable feedback.
skill.summary: Evaluate design effectiveness from a UX perspective. Assesses visual hierarchy, information architec...
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: Security audit complete. Static scanner flagged 17 high-severity findings for 'weak cryptographic algorithm', but manual review confirms all are FALSE POSITIVES. The scanner misinterprets words containing 'crit' (critique, description, critical) as cryptographic references. This is a legitimate UX design critique skill with no cryptographic code, no network access, no filesystem operations, and no external commands. No security concerns identified.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Scanner incorrectly flagged words containing 'crit' (critique, description, critical) as weak cryptographic algorithms. Manual review confirms no cryptographic code exists in this file.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Text analysis confirms all flagged lines contain only design critique content - words like 'critique', 'description', 'critical' are being misinterpreted as cryptographic terms. No actual cryptographic code present.

high_findings.title: System Reconnaissance (FALSE POSITIVE)
high_findings.description: Scanner incorrectly flagged 'The test' phrase as system reconnaissance. This refers to design evaluation testing, not system scanning.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 25 contains 'The test: If you showed this to someone...' which is clearly a design evaluation question, not a system scan command.

content.value_statement: Get expert UX design feedback that identifies AI-generated patterns, visual hierarchy issues, and actionable improvements to elevate your interface.
actual_capabilities: Detect AI-generated design patterns and provide specific improvement suggestions
Evaluate visual hierarchy and identify competing elements that confuse users
Assess information architecture and navigation clarity
Analyze emotional resonance and brand alignment
Provide prioritized, actionable feedback with specific fix commands
Review typography, color usage, and whitespace decisions
limitations: Cannot evaluate interactive behavior or user testing results
Cannot access live websites - requires codebase or screenshot access
Cannot generate actual design files or code implementations
Relies on frontend-design skill for specific anti-pattern references

use_cases.title: Pre-launch Design Review
use_cases.description: Before shipping a new feature, get comprehensive feedback on the design to catch issues that may confuse users or undermine the product experience.
use_cases.target_user: Frontend developers and designers shipping new features

use_cases.title: AI Slop Detection
use_cases.description: Identify if your interface looks like generic AI-generated design from 2024-2025 and get specific guidance on how to make it feel more intentional and unique.
use_cases.target_user: Product teams wanting distinctive, non-generic interfaces

use_cases.title: Design Critique for Stakeholders
use_cases.description: Generate structured, professional critique reports that can be shared with design teams or stakeholders to drive design decisions.
use_cases.target_user: Design leads and product managers reviewing design quality

prompt_templates.title: Basic Design Critique
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the critique skill to evaluate our interface. Focus on visual hierarchy and whether the primary action is clear.

prompt_templates.title: AI Pattern Detection
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the critique skill to check if our design looks like generic AI-generated work. Look for gradient text, glassmorphism, glowing accents, and other AI tells.
prompt_templates.scenario: Checking for overused AI design patterns

prompt_templates.title: Full UX Evaluation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the critique skill to do a complete design critique. Evaluate all 10 dimensions including visual hierarchy, information architecture, emotional resonance, typography, color, and microcopy.

prompt_templates.title: Specific Area Focus
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the critique skill to evaluate the [area: login form]. Focus on discoverability, affordance, and whether users would know how to proceed.
prompt_templates.scenario: Targeted feedback on a specific component

output_examples.input: Use the critique skill to evaluate our dashboard design.
output_examples.output:
### Anti-Patterns Verdict
**Fail** - The design shows clear AI-generated indicators: gradient text on the hero section, glowing accent borders, identical card grid layout, and the classic 'hero metric' cards. These telltale signs make the interface feel generic.

### Overall Impression
The dashboard is functional but lacks character. It follows every AI-generated template exactly. The biggest opportunity is breaking away from the pack.

### What's Working
1. Clear navigation placement in the sidebar
2. Good use of whitespace in the content area
3. Consistent button styling

### Priority Issues
1. **AI Slop Aesthetic** - Makes brand seem unoriginal. Fix: Remove gradient text, reduce glow effects, use fewer cards.
2. **Competing Hero Metrics** - Four identical metric cards create visual noise. Fix: Focus on single key metric.
3. **Generic Typography** - System fonts undermine premium positioning. Fix: Add distinctive headline font.

output_examples.input: Use the critique skill to check for AI-generated design patterns in our landing page.
output_examples.output:
### Anti-Patterns Verdict
**Pass** - While clean, the design avoids obvious AI tells. No gradient text, no glassmorphism, no hero metrics. It feels designed rather than generated.

### Priority Issues
1. **Weak Primary Action** - The CTA button blends with secondary actions. Fix: Increase size and use contrasting color.
2. **Navigation Overload** - Eight nav items compete for attention. Fix: Reduce to 5 max.

best_practices: Provide specific file paths or screenshots for the skill to evaluate
Focus on one area at a time for more actionable feedback
Use the frontend-design skill alongside critique for detailed principles
Prioritize the top 3 issues - attempting to fix everything leads to poor results
anti_patterns: Asking for critique without providing design context or files
Ignoring the AI slop detection section - this catches the most common modern design problem
Accepting all feedback without considering brand context - some 'issues' may be intentional design choices
Requesting fixes in the same prompt - critique and fixing are separate workflows

faq.question: What is the AI slop detection section?
faq.answer: This is the most critical check. It identifies if your interface looks like generic AI-generated designs from 2024-2025, which can make your product feel unoriginal and cheap.

faq.question: Does this skill write code?
faq.answer: No. The critique skill evaluates and provides feedback. Use other skills like implement or refactor to make actual changes.

faq.question: What files can I provide for critique?
faq.answer: You can provide HTML/CSS files, screenshots, or describe the interface. The skill works best with actual code or visual references.

faq.question: How is this different from the frontend-design skill?
faq.answer: The frontend-design skill provides design principles and anti-patterns as reference. The critique skill actively evaluates your specific interface against those principles.

faq.question: Can I use this for mobile designs?
faq.answer: Yes. While optimized for web interfaces, the critique skill can evaluate mobile designs, apps, and other digital interfaces.

faq.question: How do I prioritize the feedback?
faq.answer: The skill orders issues by impact. Focus on fixing the top 3 issues first. Addressing everything at once typically leads to poor results.
